svg.title: ACD routing decision — parallel input tracks
svg.desc: Three parallel input tracks feed into the ACD routing decision: calling number leading to ANI and CRM lookup, number dialed plus IVR choice leading to intent tag, and live queue conditions. These converge into priority scoring, then skills match, then agent routing.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the calling phone number feed into ACD routing?')
sendPrompt('What is ANI and how does it work in a contact center?')
sendPrompt('What CRM data does the ACD use when routing a call?')
sendPrompt('How does IVR authentication status affect ACD routing?')
sendPrompt('How does the number a customer dials affect routing?')
sendPrompt('How does what a caller does in the IVR affect routing?')
sendPrompt('What is an IVR intent tag and how does it affect ACD routing?')
sendPrompt('How does the channel — voice, chat, email — affect ACD routing?')
sendPrompt('How do live queue conditions affect ACD routing decisions?')
sendPrompt('How does agent occupancy affect ACD routing?')
sendPrompt('What are overflow rules in a contact center ACD?')
sendPrompt('How does priority scoring work in ACD routing?')
sendPrompt('How does skills-based routing work in an ACD?')
sendPrompt('What happens when a matching agent is available in the ACD?')
sendPrompt('What happens when no matching agent is available?')
